title: Behind the Scenes at the 98th Academy Awards
summary: The 98th Academy Awards, held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood, showcased a mix of emotional speeches and comedic moments, with host Conan O'Brien targeting actor Timothée Chalamet. Chalamet, who lost the best actor award to Michael B Jordan, became the subject of jokes after his comments about ballet and opera. O'Brien humorously referenced security concerns from these communities, prompting a smiling reaction from Chalamet alongside girlfriend Kylie Jenner. Later, O'Brien attempted to smooth things over by stating they were 'vibing.' Beyond the televised show, the event featured enhanced security due to international tensions, including layered perimeters and police presence. Backstage, winners like a Frankenstein production designer celebrated with Oscars and drinks, while a historic tie occurred in the best short action film category. This article provides an insider look at the unseen elements of Hollywood's premier night, highlighting both the glamour and logistical efforts involved.

background_read: The Academy Awards, commonly known as the Oscars, is an annual ceremony recognizing excellence in the film industry, organized by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences. Held in Hollywood, it attracts global attention with live broadcasts. The event combines awards presentations with entertainment, often featuring hosts who provide comedic commentary. Security is a critical aspect, especially in light of international events, to ensure safety for high-profile attendees. This article from BBC Entertainment covers the 98th edition, emphasizing humorous interactions and logistical details not typically visible to TV audiences, offering a deeper understanding of the production and atmosphere.
Article_Structure: This article analyzes the 98th Academy Awards, focusing on main points like Conan O'Brien's jokes targeting Timothée Chalamet and enhanced security due to global conflicts. Its purpose is to provide an insider perspective on unseen Oscars moments, blending entertainment with real-world context. Evidence includes direct quotes from O'Brien and descriptions of backstage activities, though it relies on observational reporting without in-depth sources. Author credibility stems from BBC Entertainment's reputation, but the piece is more descriptive than analytical. Methodology involves summarizing event highlights and security measures, offering a balanced view of the glamour and practicalities of Hollywood's biggest night.

perspectives.description: Highlights how entertainment news captures both on-screen and off-screen elements of major events.
